title: Research Proposal: A Primary-School Reading Scheme

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: Let's look at your research proposal. You want to test whether a daily twenty-minute reading session improves children's vocabulary. It's a nice, focused idea. Where are you struggling?

turns.speaker: Grace
turns.text: Mainly with the design. We were going to give every child in one class the reading sessions and then just test them at the end. But we realised we'd have nothing to compare against.

turns.speaker: Omar
turns.text: Right — without a comparison group, any improvement could just be the children getting older and picking up words naturally. We'd never know if the scheme itself did anything.

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: That's exactly the flaw. You need a control group — a second class that carries on as normal. Then you compare the two. But be careful: the two classes must be genuinely similar to start with.

turns.speaker: Grace
turns.text: Yes, we talked about that. If one class already had stronger readers, the comparison would be meaningless. So we want to test everyone's vocabulary first, before the scheme begins.

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: A baseline test — good. Now, I have a couple of worries about your proposal. Let me flag them and you tell me how you'd respond. First, twenty minutes a day for a whole term is a big commitment for busy teachers.

turns.speaker: Omar
turns.text: That's true. Our answer is that a teaching assistant, not the class teacher, would run the sessions, so it doesn't add to the teacher's workload.

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: Sensible. My second worry is the test itself. A vocabulary test can be stressful for young children, and stress affects performance.

turns.speaker: Grace
turns.text: We thought about that too. Instead of a formal written test, we'd use a picture-based game, so it feels like play rather than an exam.

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: Excellent — that's a thoughtful solution. Now, one thing you haven't mentioned: how will you get permission? These are young children.

turns.speaker: Omar
turns.text: We'd need written consent from the parents, and we'd also explain it to the children in simple terms and let them opt out if they didn't want to take part.

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: Good, that covers the ethics. Now let's divide the preparation. There's the consent paperwork, the picture-game design, and the statistical plan. Who wants what?

turns.speaker: Grace
turns.text: I'll design the picture game — I've worked with this age group before, so I know what holds their attention.

turns.speaker: Omar
turns.text: And I'll prepare the consent paperwork and the letters to parents. Admin doesn't bother me.

turns.speaker: Dr Naylor
turns.text: Then I'll draft the statistical plan, since that's the part you're least sure about. Have the whole proposal ready for the ethics committee by the end of the month.

turns.speaker: Omar
turns.text: End of the month, understood. Thank you — the control-group point especially. I don't think our original design would have proved anything at all.

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: What was the main flaw in the students' original design?

options.text: It had no group to compare results against.
It tested too many children at once.
It lasted only a single week.

questionGroups.prompt: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questionGroups.instructions_html: Why do the students want to test the children before the scheme starts?

options.text: to practise using the test equipment
to check the two classes are similar at the start
to decide which children can read

questionGroups.instructions_html: Which TWO concerns does the tutor raise about the proposal?

options.text: the stress a test may cause children
the length of the school term

questions.prompt_text: Two concerns raised by Dr Naylor

questionGroups.prompt: Who will prepare each part of the proposal?
questionGroups.instructions_html: Choose your answer from the list of people A–C and select the correct letter for each part.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.prompt_text: The class that carries on as normal is called the ______ group.
To reduce stress, vocabulary will be tested with a ______ game.
The sessions will be run by a teaching ______, not the class teacher.
